rulings.Case Title: ITC FINANCIAL LICENSES, INC. VS. STAN SCHULDINER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 8, 2019, Line 1, RESPONDENT STAN SCHULDINER Mtn To Tax Costs Cross-complainant Schuldiner's motion to tax is DENIED. The Court of Appeal determined that "Office Depot shall recover its costs on appeal." (Unpublished Opinion, pg. 17.) Office Depot's verified costs memorandum, which seeks a mere $590, is prima facie evidence that the listed costs were necessarily incurred and Office Depot did not need to attach all of its attendant bills. (See Bach v. City of Butte (1989) 215 Cal.App.3d 294, 308.) The costs that Office Depot seeks for filing fees, preparing the reporter's transcript, copying, and transmitting papers are expressly recoverable per CRC 8.278(d)(1)(A), (B), (D), and (E). Schuldiner's objection that the redactions cause confusion fails. Office Depot redacted unrelated costs to reduce confusion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Office Depot is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: JERMAINE HORTON VS. FCA US LLC.,A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 8, 2019, Line 5, DEFENDANT FCA US LLC, SAN LEANDRO CAR STOP, LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Defendants FCA US, LLC and San Leandro Car Stop, LLC's motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff Jermaine Horton's fraudulent concealment cause of action and punitive damages prayer is GRANTED. Defendants shift the burden of production to plaintiff if they "present evidence that he does not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, needed evidence - as through admissions by the plaintiff following extensive discovery to the effect that he has discovered nothing." (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 854-55.) Defendants have shown via plaintiff's discovery responses his lack of admissible evidence that FCA US knew of a defect in the totally integrated power module before plaintiff bought his vehicle in May 2013. Defendants thus shifted the evidentiary burden to plaintiff. George Semaan-an associate in a law firm-lacks the expertise, personal knowledge and foundation to interpret the materials proffered with his declaration. Defendants' evidentiary objections are therefore SUSTAINED. (Evid. Code §§403, 702.) Plaintiff offers no expert declaration to interpret the technical documents and email discussions among engineers to explain how they support plaintiff's fraudulent concealment claim. Absent clear and convincing evidence of fraud, the punitive damages prayer fails too. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ERIC B. WALL DDS VS. THE CANADA LIFE ASSURANCE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Canada Life Assurance Company To Produce Documents And Further Written Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two, Regarding Its In-House Doctor Carla Morton, M.D.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, February 8, 2019, Line 1, (Part 1 of 2 for tentative ruling) PLAINTIFF ERIC WALL's Motion To Compel Canada Life Assurance Company To Produce Documents And Further Written Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two, Regarding Its In-House Doctor Carla Morton, M.D. Pro Tem Judge Jason Yurasek, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion to compel is granted, in part. The records requested in response to Request for Production No. 26 is calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. Therefore Canada Life's relevance objections to the Request is overruled. Canada Life's objections to burden and privacy, however, are well-taken. As to burden, the request that Canada Life produce certain records from Dr. Carla Morton from 2010 to the present may be unduly burdensome. For example, if what Canada Life says is true (namely that it would take hundreds of hours and nearly $100,000 in expenses to collected, review and produce relevant records), it would be burdensome. But, what is not clear, is whether manual collection and review processes is the only way to obtain responsive materials. Therefore, Canada Life is ordered to search electronically for records in response to Request for Production No. 26 (whether by itself, its record management company, Dr. Morton herself, any other individual or entity within its control, or any combination thereof). If for some reason an electronic search of records is not possible (which shall be verified to counsel for Plaintiff by declaration of the responding party), Canada Life shall review and produce the first 12 reports it identifies in reviewing reports from January 1, 2010 that respond to Request for Production 26. That review shall be in chronological order from 2010 onward to the present until 12 responsive reports are identified and produced, or after completion of a review of files dated on or before January 1, 2013, or the review of 100 files, whichever is reached first. Finally, as to privacy, Canada Life may redact documents it produces in response to Request for Production No. 26 to protect the privacy rights of third-parties. No other redactions shall be permitted without leave of Court. The parties to submit (via email to jyjudgeprotem@gmail.com ) a proposed order agreed as to form for the Court to sign. =(302/JPT)
